summary: Scorching Sugaree and marathon Scarlet>Fire jam dominate the night
blurb: The show bursts with a fiery Sugaree and a monumental Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain sequence that fuels an explosive second set. Highlights include Bird Song, The Wheel, Wharf Rat and a soaring 20‑minute Playing In The Band. Overall the performance is high‑energy and tightly locked in.
review: On October 15, 1984 at the Hartford Civic Center the Dead delivered one of their most electrifying late‑'80s outings. A blistering Sugaree opened the first set, leading into a tight run that featured Bird Song and Jack Straw before the band dove headfirst into an unforgettable Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain marathon – a 29‑minute jam that showcases Jerry’s virtuosic leads and Phil’s “fire on the mountain” bass lines. The second set kept the momentum rolling with a powerful The Wheel, a gritty Wharf Rat, and an epic >20‑minute Playing In The Band that sent the audience into a frenzy. The encore’s gentle rendition of It’s All Over Now Baby Blue provided a perfect wind‑down after such high‑octane improvisation. Across multiple audience and matrix sources the band sounds tight, energetic, and in top form – a must‑listen for fans of this era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across five of six recordings (all describing the show as high‑energy, with standout Sugaree, Scarlet>Fire jam, and epic Playing In The Band). Only one mixed review noted a meandering moment but did not detract from overall praise.

best_recording.reason: Warm audience tape with good clarity, captures the crowd ambience and all key moments; consistently cited as the top source for this show.

key_highlights: Marathon Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain jam (≈29 min) that drives the second set.
Sugaree delivers a scorching opening with Garcia’s lead work on fire.
Epic >20‑minute Playing In The Band showcases the band at peak improvisational flow.
Strong recording ambience on audience tapes while matrix capture adds clarity.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s lead work is virtuoso – fierce licks in the Scarlet>Fire jam, powerful vocal delivery on Sugaree and driving improvisation throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh lays down fire‑like bass lines that underpin the extended jams, especially noticeable during the Scarlet>Fire sequence and The Wheel.
band_performance.Bob: Weir adds heavy, raunchy distortion and even whammy‑bar fireworks, giving extra grit to the rhythm section.
band_performance.Mickey: Energetic drumming that locks in with Bill, pushing the band forward during The Wheel, Space, and the high‑octane jams.
band_performance.Billy: Tight, fun percussion that complements Mickey’s grooves, driving the momentum especially in the second set climaxes.
